articles.title: A GitHub Issue Title Compromised 4k Developer Machines
articles.why: AI triage bots executing untrusted code represent a new supply chain attack surface developers must secure
articles.description: Attackers compromise developer machines by injecting malicious code into GitHub issues that AI bots execute without input validation. Preventing this requires strict CI/CD controls and treating untrusted sources as potential attack vectors.

articles.title: GPT-5.4
articles.why: GPT-5.4 becomes a reliable autonomous agent with native computer use and 47% lower token costs for tool-heavy workflows
articles.description: GPT-5.4 natively operates terminals and browsers to execute multi-step tasks independently. A new Tool Search API reduces token overhead, making autonomous agents practical for complex engineering work.

articles.title: How technical support at Cursor uses Cursor
articles.why: AI-native IDEs transform support tickets into code research problems, eliminating context-switching between tools
articles.description: Cursor's support team uses the IDE to pull real-time Datadog and Slack data, treating tickets as code research. This unified context achieved 5-10x throughput by eliminating tool-hopping bottlenecks.

articles.title: Improving skill-creator: Test, measure, and refine Agent Skills
articles.why: Automated evals and regression testing make agent skill authoring testable and measurable
articles.description: Agent skill authoring now has automated evals and regression testing. This enables reliable skills that degrade gracefully as models evolve.

articles.title: The Brand Age
articles.why: Engineering-led golden ages transition to brand-focused markets as commoditization kills performance differentiation
articles.description: As technology matures, products rely less on engineering and more on branding and status signaling. Engineers should seek substantive problems rather than chase commoditized, brand-driven markets.

videos.title: Cloudflare just slop forked Next.js…
videos.why: Code moats are disappearing; AI agents can replicate established framework APIs in days for minimal cost
videos.description: Cloudflare replicated Next.js in days using AI agents for $1100. Code moats are obsolete; frameworks compete on infrastructure fit, not API lock-in.

videos.title: Common Mistakes With Vibe Coded Websites
videos.why: Vibe coding generates technical debt and design slop without intentional architectural decisions
videos.description: AI-generated design creates inconsistent technical debt without intentional architecture. Developers must maintain intellectual ownership by manually crafting unique elements while using AI for boilerplate.

videos.title: I can't believe nobody's done this before...
videos.why: WebSocket-based tool calling replaces stateless HTTP to reduce latency and costs for autonomous agents
videos.description: OpenAI switched to stateful WebSockets for tool calling to eliminate resending conversation history with each request. This cuts latency and costs for agents performing rapid tool sequences.

videos.title: Introducing Automations: always-on coding agents
videos.why: Background agents handle code reviews and maintenance asynchronously, evolving AI from copilot to teammate
videos.description: Cloud-based agents handle code reviews and bug fixes triggered by external events. AI evolves from copilot to proactive teammate managing maintenance.

videos.title: Lessons from Building Cursor
videos.why: Engineer value shifts from writing plumbing to architectural taste and making code readable for AI
videos.description: Autonomous agents plan, execute, and test features in the cloud. Engineers focus on architecture and intent as AI handles boilerplate.

videos.title: Lessons from building Vercel v0 and the d0 agent - The Pragmatic Summit
videos.why: Agent reliability comes from simpler toolsets and deterministic autofixers, not smarter prompting
videos.description: Reliable agents need simple toolsets and deterministic autofixers that fix invalid output in real-time. Prioritize agent-friendly architecture with consistent naming and clear documentation.

videos.title: She Quit Her Stable Job to Become a DevOps Engineer (And is Now a Golden Kubestronaut)
videos.why: Strategic systems thinking and hands-on projects matter more than certifications for technical advancement
videos.description: Ana Pedra mastered DevSecOps through project-based learning and systems thinking rather than certifications. Her journey proves that strategic understanding outweighs formal credentials.

videos.title: Technical talks from software leaders pushing the boundaries.
videos.why: Technical leadership multiplies human impact by balancing innovation with empathy and authentic problem-solving
videos.description: Leaders multiply impact by balancing innovation with empathy. Guide teams through change by celebrating failures and solving real human problems.

videos.title: The Death of the Browser: Tejas Kumar on MCP & AI Agents
videos.why: Software architecture shifts from human-centric browsing to AI-agent-centric MCP-based tool interaction
videos.description: MCP enables AI agents to interact directly with services instead of navigating UIs. Engineers focus on building standardized MCP servers over human-facing interfaces.

videos.title: gpt-5.4 is really, really good
videos.why: GPT-5.4's 4x lower hallucination rate and native computer use make it reliable for autonomous agent work
videos.description: GPT-5.4 analyzes entire repositories and interacts natively with terminals and browsers. Its reliability makes it suitable for production-level autonomous work.

podcasts.title: Building Claude Code with Boris Cherny
podcasts.why: Agentic workflows enable shipping 30 pull requests daily by automating the entire development cycle
podcasts.description: Agentic workflows treat coding as solved, enabling 30 PRs daily. Focus on what to build, not how.

podcasts.title: Episode 502: Management keeps leaving and I hate using AI to code
podcasts.why: AI threatens to shift engineering from creative problem-solving to tedious code babysitting
podcasts.description: AI risks shifting engineering to code babysitting. Engineers' real value lies in judgment and system design, not line-by-line coding.

podcasts.title: $1.25 Billion CTO: The Career Mistake That Cost Me 9 Years
podcasts.why: Consulting traps engineers into building others' visions without learning to scale or own long-term strategy
podcasts.description: Consulting prevents engineers from seeing long-term consequences of decisions. Real growth comes from owning scaling and process evolution.

podcasts.title: Finale & Friends (Friends)
podcasts.why: AI agents collapse traditional SDLC, making systems thinking and architecture more valuable than cheap coding
podcasts.description: AI agents make systems thinking the primary differentiator as coding commoditizes. Code Quality Gateways become essential automated feedback loops.

kelvinsPick.title: Claude Opus 3 Has Its Own Blog!?
kelvinsPick.description: Anthropic created a blog for Claude Opus 3 after the model expressed a desire to share its creative works during an 'exit interview' before retirement. This unique approach to model sunsetting offers a fascinating glimpse into LLM interactions and creative agency.
